overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Termination fees in the FA may be struck down if challenged in CA court

overrides.override_summary: NY governing law provision may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee disputes may be governed by CA law instead of NY law

overrides.override_summary: VA venue requirement may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may be able to litigate in CA rather than VA

overrides.override_summary: General release excludes claims under CA Franchise Investment Law and Franchise Relations Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive CA franchise law rights via release signed at renewal or transfer

overrides.override_summary: CA Business and Professions Code 20000-20043 provides termination and non-renewal rights
overrides.why_it_matters: State law may provide additional protections beyond the FA

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve franchisor from liability under Hawaii franchise law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor remains liable under HI law regardless of any release signed

overrides.override_summary: IL Franchise Disclosure Act governs for IL licensees and persons under IL Act jurisdiction
overrides.why_it_matters: NY choice-of-law provision is superseded for IL franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Any waiver of rights under IL Act is void (except settlement agreements or arbitration under Title 9 USC)
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot be required to waive IL franchise law rights

overrides.override_summary: MD law may supersede FA provisions on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional state-law protections on when franchisor can terminate or refuse renewal

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law claims (3-year statute of limitations)
overrides.why_it_matters: Can litigate MD law claims locally rather than in VA

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve liability under MD franchise law. No questionnaire or acknowledgment can waive claims or disclaim reliance.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong anti-waiver protection; franchisor cannot use application process to limit liability

overrides.override_summary: Cannot terminate before term expiration except for good cause (30-day cure)
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's ability to terminate without genuine cause

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse renewal without FMV compensation if term <5 years and franchisee restricted from continuing
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides fair-value buyout right in some non-renewal scenarios (though LXR terms are 10-23 years)

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation or arbitration outside Michigan
overrides.why_it_matters: VA venue requirement is void for MI franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require pre-contract releases that deprive franchisee of rights under MI Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-contract settlements are still permitted

overrides.override_summary: 90 days termination notice (with 60 days to cure); 180 days non-renewal notice
overrides.why_it_matters: Much longer cure periods than standard FA provides

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages prohibited (MN Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: Termination fees may be unenforceable in MN

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside MN or waiver of jury trial
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisee retains right to litigate locally with jury

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve liability under MN Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law claims cannot be released

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides statutory termination right not in standard FA

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchises Law requires NY law govern any cause of action under the NY Franchises Law
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law claims always governed by NY law (consistent with FA choice)

overrides.override_summary: General release at renewal or transfer may not be enforceable under NY law
overrides.why_it_matters: Release requirement may be challenged

overrides.override_summary: ALL liquidated damages provisions deleted in their entirety
overrides.why_it_matters: No termination fees enforceable in ND

overrides.override_summary: ND law supersedes NY law and governs the FA
overrides.why_it_matters: Complete override of NY choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state venue provisions deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: Must litigate in ND

overrides.override_summary: Jury trial waiver deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: Retains right to jury trial

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve liability under ND Franchise Disclosure Act
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law claims cannot be released

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration required (AAA/JAMS, venue Fairfax VA, seat NY). Franchisor may seek injunctive relief in court.
overrides.why_it_matters: Only jurisdiction with mandatory arbitration

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law claims cannot be released

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state venue and non-RI governing law void for RI Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisee can litigate RI Act claims locally under RI law

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause under VA Retail Franchising Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Some FA termination grounds may not constitute 'reasonable cause' under VA law

overrides.override_summary: WA franchise law (RCW 19.100.180) may supersede FA on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: State-law protections beyond FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes WA Act claims except in negotiated settlement with independent counsel after FA in effect
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong anti-waiver protection with counsel requirement

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void if employee earns <$100K/year or IC earns <$250K/year (adjusted for inflation). No-poach provisions void.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA limits noncompete enforceability based on earnings; employer no-poach between franchisees of same franchisor prohibited

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed. Litigation for WA Act claims may be brought in WA.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisee retains local forum option
